text: auto-push on every mutating command is a footgun in offline/slow-network environments and adds latency to every write. If implemented, must be opt-in via config (e.g. gittoc.autopush=true). Also: T-65 (default remote) is a prerequisite — no point auto-pushing without knowing which remote to use.

T-65 (default remote) is now closed. This is unblocked from a dependency standpoint. Design still needs thought: opt-in config flag (gittoc.autopush=true), must handle network failures gracefully (don't block the local mutation if push fails), and should probably auto-pull before mutating to reduce conflicts.

Before adding auto-push/pull, T-80 may be the safer precursor: first make concurrent-edit failures easy to inspect and recover from locally, then decide whether automation is still necessary. Auto-sync without good visibility risks hiding the actual failure mode.

Concrete implementation sketch: use git config 'gittoc.autopush' (bool, default false) read via 'git config --get gittoc.autopush'. When true, every mutating command (new, update, dep, note, claim, close, reject) appends an auto-pull before the mutation and an auto-push after the commit. Network failures on push should warn to stderr but not fail the command (local mutation is still valid). Pull conflicts should abort the mutation with a clear message pointing to 'gittoc pull' for manual resolution. Enable with: git config gittoc.autopush true.
I like 'gittoc.autopush'. We could add a commandline-flag to 'setup' to enable it. But not in the default one-liner in the README.
